title: Rosa × cimelium
taxonomy: Rosa × cimelium is classified as a hybrid of the Rosa damascena and Rosa moschata, specifically a type of Damask rose. This classification is based on its parentage and the resulting characteristics of the plant. As a member of the Rosa genus, Rosa × cimelium belongs to the family Rosaceae. The exact classification of Rosa × cimelium may vary depending on the source, but it is generally recognized as a hybrid within the larger classification of Damask roses. Rosa × cimelium's classification is also influenced by its geographic distribution and the specific cultivars that exist.
uses: Rosa × cimelium, also known as the Cramoisi Superieur, is a type of rose that has been used in traditional medicine. The flowers and leaves of this plant have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. In addition, Rosa × cimelium has been used as a treatment for skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant's flowers and leaves are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to their medicinal uses. Further research is needed to fully understand the extent of Rosa × cimelium's medicinal benefits.
benefits: Rosa × cimelium is a type of rose that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in the plant have been known to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain. Rosa × cimelium has also been used to treat digestive issues, such as constipation and diarrhea, due to its ability to regulate gut health. Additionally, the plant has been used to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels, making it a potential treatment for cardiovascular disease. The flavonoids in Rosa × cimelium may also have antimicrobial properties, which can help protect against infections.
properties: Rosa × cimelium, also known as the Celsiana rose, is a rose species with a long history of being used in traditional medicine. Its therapeutic properties are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. These compounds are believed to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. In traditional medicine, the Rosa × cimelium has been used to treat various conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems. Further research is needed to fully understand its therapeutic potential and confirm its effectiveness in treating specific health conditions.
constituents: Rosa × cimelium, also known as the César Gérald rose, contains active constituents such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. These compounds contribute to its potential health benefits. Flavonoids present in Rosa × cimelium may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Phenolic acids are also known for their antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. However, further research is needed to fully understand the specific effects of these active constituents.
parts: Rosa × cimelium is a hybrid rose species, and research suggests its medicinal properties come from its flowers and leaves. The flowers are rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The leaves of Rosa × cimelium contain salicylic acid, a compound with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's flowers and leaves are also used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including skin conditions and fever. However, further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of Rosa × cimelium for medicinal purposes.
preparations: Rosa × cimelium, also known as the hybrid tea rose, is not commonly used in herbal preparations due to its hybrid nature and lack of traditional medicinal properties. However, some rose varieties, including Rosa × cimelium, may be used to make herbal teas and infusions for their potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The petals of Rosa × cimelium can be used to create a fragrant and soothing tea, which may help to reduce stress and promote relaxation. Additionally, rose hips from Rosa × cimelium, the fruit of the rose plant, are rich in vitamin C and may be used to make jams, jellies, and herbal remedies. Despite its potential uses, the plant's hybrid nature makes it less suitable for traditional herbal medicine.
side_effects: Rosa × cimelium, commonly known as the Cinnamon Rose, is a hybrid rose variety that may cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, or respiratory problems in some individuals due to its strong fragrance and potential allergens. People with sensitive skin or allergies should exercise caution when handling or coming into contact with the plant. The rose's thorns can also cause puncture wounds or scratches, leading to minor bleeding or bruising. In rare cases, ingesting any part of the plant may cause gastrointestinal upset or other adverse effects. It is essential to wear protective clothing and follow proper handling procedures when working with the Cinnamon Rose.
